topic: Verification — Van Camp DOI annotation (ISS-1944) post-patch; confirm no new citation exposure introduced
change_summary: Decider self-applied ISS-1944 patch: annotated Van Camp 2001 DOI as unverifiable in WIN-013 detail_evidence (commit 2916dad, 2026-05-11T05:32 UTC). Follow-up commit 2d76e6a (2026-05-11T06:05 UTC) widened tldr_evidence magnetic-perturbation range from ~5–10 nT to ~5–15 nT to match detail prose (closes c5 hole #4).
our_argument_summary: Membach SG 1999 null is cited by dome as confirming, but applying dome's own κ=1.677 nT/µGal to the documented 1999 European magnetic perturbation (multi-site 5–15 nT) predicts a contact-phase Δg of order a few µGal — well above the SG noise floor (0.01–0.1 µGal). The SG recorded zero. Verdict: Refuted by Data. Defense-neutralization carve-outs are addressed structurally in Part 6.14 (κ Coupling Cluster) as a vise: every carve-out exempting 1999 from κ also destroys PRED-TIER-3's 10–13 µGal August 2026 derivation.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: monitor.py contains no Membach domain and no SG-null verification logic for WIN-013. Domain 7 tautologically validates κ=1.67 against itself (KAPPA hardcoded; kappa_obs hardcoded as 10.9/6.5 = 1.677). No data fetch from any SG archive or BGS/INTERMAGNET endpoint.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Hardcoded identity check for κ in monitor.py Domain 7; no monitor.py code targets WIN-013 specifically.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Dome live-site escape clauses (a) Chebychev low-pass attenuates contact-phase transients before Level-3 release; (b) measurements were not optimized for C1/C4 timing correlation. Both are addressed in current detail_evidence and rebutted structurally in WIN-012 / Part 6.14.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new. Domain #7 vs dashboard Domain 5 numbering mismatch (ISS-1946) remains open and out of scope for this verification.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py has no Membach domain, no Van Camp data fetch, no SG-null verification. Code analysis unchanged from c5.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Unchanged from c5. Standard physics does not predict an eclipse SG signal at Membach in 1999; dome is making its own non-standard prediction via κ.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged from c5. Van Camp Membach SG observation (Aug 1999) was publicly indexed ~25 years before dome cited it.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from c5. 0.0 µGal is an adopted observation, not derived from dome geometry.

holes_found.description: Internal range/prediction inconsistency introduced by the ISS-1944 follow-up patch (commit 2d76e6a, 2026-05-11T06:05 UTC). The c5 hole #4 flagged tldr_evidence saying '~5–10 nT' while detail prose said '5–15 nT'. The patch resolved that mismatch by widening tldr_evidence to '~5–15 nT' — but it did NOT update the predicted gravity range, which remains '~3–6 µGal' in BOTH tldr_evidence and detail_evidence. Through dome's own κ = 1.677 nT/µGal, a ΔB envelope of 5–15 nT translates to Δg = 5/1.677 ≈ 2.98 µGal up to 15/1.677 ≈ 8.94 µGal — i.e. ~3–9 µGal, not ~3–6 µGal. The prose now conflates a 5–15 nT magnetic envelope (cross-study: Strestík ΔY≈+10/ΔX≈−5; Hvoždara ΔX=−5 to −8, ΔY=+12 to +15; Curto 2006) with a 3–6 µGal gravity prediction that only matches the narrower 5–10 nT subrange. A dome defender can correctly observe: 'Your own range and your own κ predict 3–9 µGal at Membach; you arbitrarily compressed the upper bound to 6 µGal.' The rhetorical force of 'well above 0.01–0.1 µGal SG noise floor' is unaffected (6 vs 9 µGal both crush a 0.1 µGal floor), but the arithmetic looks sloppy and a calculator-armed reviewer finds the discrepancy in 30 seconds.
holes_found.recommendation: Pick ONE of two patches. (a) Widen the Δg prediction to ~3–9 µGal in BOTH tldr_evidence and detail_evidence — change '3–6 µGal' to '3–9 µGal' in the two locations (tldr_evidence final clause; detail_evidence third paragraph final sentence 'Through κ, that ΔB predicts a contact-phase Δg ≈ 3–6 µGal at Membach'). OR (b) Keep '3–6 µGal' and add a short clarifying clause to detail_evidence anchoring it to the closest geomagnetic-latitude analog: 'Strestík's ΔY ≈ +10 / ΔX ≈ −5 nT (Czech observatories) is the closest geomagnetic-latitude analog to Membach (Czech-Belgian baseline ~600 km); through κ, that 5–10 nT subrange predicts a contact-phase Δg ≈ 3–6 µGal at Membach. The broader 5–15 nT envelope across Czech/Slovak observatories implies up to ~9 µGal at the highest-perturbation sites, none of which were Membach.' Option (b) is more defensible because magnetic perturbations were geographically heterogeneous in 1999 and a Membach-specific prediction should anchor to the closest analog; option (a) is simpler and equally honest. Either is acceptable. Decider can self-apply.

stronger_arguments.description: The ISS-1944 patch annotates the Van Camp 2001 DOI as unverifiable but keeps the citation visible with the broken DOI as a link. This is the right call — it preserves the dome's claimed source for reader verification while flagging the resolution failure. Consider adding one short clause to pre-empt a defender accusing us of citing a non-existent paper: e.g. 'We retain the dome's cited DOI for reader verification despite the 404 — the underlying 1999 Membach SG eclipse measurement is documented in Van Camp's subsequent work (e.g., DOI 10.1029/2006JB004405) and the multi-site magnetic record stands independently of the headline DOI.' This is purely defensive; not required.
sources: Van Camp 2006 hydrogeology DOI 10.1029/2006JB004405 (a verifiable Van Camp Membach paper, suggested as alternative anchor in the c5 summary_for_decider)
Strestík 2001 and Curto 2006 (independent magnetic-field confirmation of the 1999 perturbation range, unaffected by Van Camp DOI exposure)

kernel_of_truth.description: The Membach SG null is a real measurement (data/wins.json:013/finding) and the Van Camp et al. group at the Royal Observatory of Belgium is a real, long-running Membach SG team. The dome's instinct to cite the most-cited 1999 SG paper as the empirical anchor is reasonable. The dome's error is a DOI string that the publishing house's prefix does not support: Phys. Earth Planet. Inter. is Elsevier (prefix 10.1016), not Springer/Kluwer (10.1023) per monitor/decisions/open-issues.json:ISS-1944. Annotating the unresolvable DOI rather than removing the citation entirely preserves reader access to the dome's source intent while flagging the resolution failure.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The DOI being unverifiable does not change the SG null itself. The 5–15 nT magnetic perturbation range cited in WIN-013 detail_evidence rests on Strestík 2001 (Springer chapter), Hvoždara & Prigancová 2002 (ISS-1947 open — ResearchGate-only), and Curto et al. 2006 (DOI 10.1029/2005JA011499). Even if all Van Camp 2001 citation exposure were removed, the κ × ΔB-through-the-equation prediction of a 3–6 µGal (or 3–9 µGal per the moderate finding above) contact-phase Δg, contrasted with the Membach SG's null at 0.01–0.1 µGal precision, still refutes the dome claim. The verdict 'Refuted by Data' is robust to citation exposure on the Van Camp anchor — the κ math kills it.

deeper_analysis:
Verification of the two-step patch sequence on WIN-013. Commit 2916dad (2026-05-11T05:32 UTC) annotated the Van Camp 2001 DOI as unverifiable in WIN-013 detail_evidence — closes ISS-1944 in the WIN-013 locus. Commit 2d76e6a (2026-05-11T06:05 UTC) widened tldr_evidence magnetic range from ~5–10 nT to ~5–15 nT — closes c5 hole #4. Both patches are content-appropriate and preserve the verdict. The second patch, however, exposes a residual arithmetic inconsistency: the 3–6 µGal prediction now mismatches the 5–15 nT input range through κ = 1.677 — that is the moderate finding above. Two fixes proposed; decider can self-apply.

Known open issues NOT in this verification's scope:
- ISS-1945 (Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018 URL→wrong paper) affects WIN-014 + section 6.14; queue_id 331 is WIN-013-only.
- ISS-1946 (Domain #7 vs dashboard Domain 5) is moderate, prose still says 'monitor.py Domain #7' — out of scope here.
- ISS-1947 (Hvoždara ResearchGate-only) is moderate-leaning-minor; Strestík + Curto independently cover the 5–15 nT range — out of scope here.

The c5 review's recommendation to consider Van Camp 2006 DOI 10.1029/2006JB004405 as an alternative anchor was NOT taken in commit 2916dad — the decider chose the more conservative annotate-but-retain path. That choice is defensible (preserves the dome's exact cited source for reader verification) and the c5 review marked the operator-aware escalation as optional. No action recommended on this thread unless operator weighs in.

Verdict assessment: current_verdict_holds=true, confidence 0.82 (up from 0.78 at c5 — ISS-1944 closure recovered ~0.10; new moderate finding offsets by ~0.06). recommended_action: minor_edit (one fix; decider can self-apply).

Strestík 2001 Springer chapter URL https://link.springer.com/chapter/10.1007/978-94-010-0800-6_55 — still resolves; cited consistently with c5

Hvoždara & Prigancová 2002 — already tracked under ISS-1947 (ResearchGate-only); not re-validated this run

summary_for_decider:
Verification class review of WIN-013 c5 patches.

PATCH VERIFIED LANDED ✓ — ISS-1944 (Van Camp 2001 DOI 404) annotated in WIN-013 detail_evidence as '(Phys. Earth Planet. Inter.; as cited by dome — DOI unverifiable: 10.1023/A:1013940928637 returns 404)'. Conservative annotate-but-retain approach is correct: preserves reader access to the dome's cited source while flagging resolution failure. The c5 alternative-anchor suggestion (Van Camp 2006 DOI 10.1029/2006JB004405) was not taken; that's defensible.

FOLLOWUP PATCH VERIFIED LANDED ✓ — tldr_evidence widened from '~5–10 nT' to '~5–15 nT' (commit 2d76e6a), closing c5 hole #4 (tldr/detail inconsistency).

NEW MODERATE FINDING (introduced by the followup patch): the gravity prediction '~3–6 µGal' in both tldr and detail now mismatches the widened ΔB range '5–15 nT' through dome's own κ = 1.677, which gives 2.98–8.94 µGal (~3–9 µGal). Two acceptable fixes in holes_found[0].recommendation; either works; decider can self-apply.

OUT OF SCOPE for this verification (tracked separately, still open): ISS-1945 (Tomás 2018 — WIN-014/SEC-6.14 scope), ISS-1946 (Domain #7 vs Domain 5 dashboard), ISS-1947 (Hvoždara ResearchGate). No new issues for these in this run.

QUEUE-ID HOUSEKEEPING (decider attention requested, NOT a WIN-013 finding): queue_id 333 (target p6-614-kappa-cluster, target_type section-new, class deep-attack) is annotated as 'Reassigned from 330 (collision with archive) on 2026-05-11T05:56:53.968339Z'. The existing review monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/SEC-6.14-kappa-cluster.json carries queue_id:330 and reviewed_at 2026-05-11T05:18:44.500Z — content review is complete but PROP-009 strict matching (review.queue_id == queue.queue_id) fails because the queue's queue_id has been rewritten 330→333. Per curmudgeon.md Step 0b strict rule the item is technically un-reviewed; per Step 5 spirit (no redundant reviews when content is covered) it is effectively reviewed. I deferred rather than write a near-identical c2 of section content reviewed <2h ago. Decider needs to either (a) manually pop queue_id 333 referencing SEC-6.14-kappa-cluster.json (queue_id 330) on the next run, or (b) clarify the reassignment policy in priority-queue schema so curmudgeon knows whether to treat queue_id changes as 'new review needed'. Will not be resolved without a decider action — the queue item will otherwise linger.
